subject: [RFC PATCH 1/3] mm/numa: add exclusive node pool and numa=standby boot parameter

2026-06-10.patch_summary: This patch introduces a new feature to the Linux kernel's NUMA (Non-Uniform Memory Access) subsystem, allowing for the creation of 'standby' nodes at boot time. These standby nodes can be claimed by drivers at runtime, enabling them to place hotplugged memory on distinct NUMA nodes without relying on BIOS-assigned proximity domains. The patch includes a new exclusive node pool and a claim/release interface for drivers to use these standby nodes. It also updates the NUMA distance table to include entries for standby nodes. This change allows for more flexibility in memory tier membership and fallback ordering, but raises concerns about dynamic addition of nodes and potential ambiguity with phys_to_target_node().
